symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt 'switch-like' onset of sustained regular rapid palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Instantaneous on/off and regularity are hallmarks of a re-entrant SVT.
Sinus tachycardia accelerates and decelerates gradually rather than switching on abruptly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic near-syncope / lightheadedness during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope during sustained tachycardia signals borderline cardiac output and drives emergency escalation.
Haemodynamic symptoms during a regular tachycardia mandate excluding VT with an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular pulse too fast to count on self-palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reported regular very fast pulse is consistent with SVT; self-palpation is unreliable for exact rate or regularity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prominent pounding sensation in the neck (frog sign / cannon a-waves)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neck pulsation from simultaneous atrial and ventricular contraction against a closed tricuspid valve is characteristic of AVNRT.
Cannon a-waves reflect AV dissociation/re-entry, not a normal sinus mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness and mild exertional dyspnoea during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rate-related reduction in coronary filling and cardiac output can cause chest tightness and breathlessness.
Sympathetic activation accompanies SVT but is also a panic-attack feature — a visceral-bias trap if over-weighted toward anxiety.
Anxiety/diaphoresis alone cannot distinguish panic from a true tachyarrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: High acute caffeine intake (two double espressos) before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stimulant load is a recognised adrenergic trigger for paroxysmal SVT and a modifiable factor.
Absence of crushing chest pain lowers but does not exclude ischaemia; does not remove the need for escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heart rate 185 bpm, perfectly regular, on clinical examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular ~185 bpm is typical of AVNRT; clinician-measured, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 110/75 — maintained despite the tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved BP in a young structurally-normal heart favours a supraventricular origin over VT — but is a clinician measurement the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible cannon a-waves / rapid neck venous pulsations on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively visible cannon a-waves support AV re-entry; a clinician sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac auscultation (no murmurs/gallops/rubs) and clear lung fields

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of murmurs and clear lungs argues against structural disease/failure; auscultation is clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG showing a regular narrow-complex tachycardia (± response to vagal manoeuvre)

investigation_details.expected_result: Regular narrow-complex tachycardia ~185 bpm; termination to sinus with a vagal manoeuvre confirms a re-entrant SVT.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish SVT from VT or capture the rhythm — the reason emergency assessment is required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ECG is the definitive discriminator between SVT and VT and is unobtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Abrupt switch-like onset + neck pounding (cannon a-waves) + a regular very rapid rate together strongly localise to nodal re-entry.
Near-syncope plus chest tightness/dyspnoea during the episode marks the presentation as an emergency rather than an outpatient palpitation work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG during the active episode (in ED / by paramedics)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Captures and classifies the rhythm and excludes VT — the pivotal investigation, achievable only in a monitored setting.
Allows safe vagal manoeuvre / adenosine administration with resuscitation backup.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline resting 12-lead ECG and outpatient Holter monitor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Post-episode work-up for pre-excitation (WPW) and recurrence; arranged by GP/cardiology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum troponin as a blanket test in the absence of ischaemic features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated purely for rate-related symptoms without ongoing ischaemic chest pain; ordered only if the ED clinical picture warrants it.
